key_insight: Token optimization for LLM-based Oracle-to-PostgreSQL migration is a multi-objective tradeoff, not just prompt shortening: some strategies cut tokens with little quality loss, while aggressive compression can severely damage semantic match.
engineering_relevance: For database migration pipelines, the best practical gains come from adaptive context management that reduces cost and preserves translation quality, rather than blindly minimizing context size.
method: Evaluated 12 token-optimization strategies for Oracle SQL/PL-SQL to PostgreSQL migration.
Compared them on 10- and 100-query samples using Valid Syntax Rate, Exact Match, Semantic Match, CodeBLEU, and Token Efficiency.
Studied approaches including pruning, minification, schema distillation, adaptive routing, and hybrid optimization.
evidence_signal: On the 100-query sample, mild context pruning kept Semantic Match near baseline (89.75% vs. 89.80%), while adaptive routing reduced input tokens by 8.72% and output tokens by 5.49% with 88.40% Semantic Match and a 6.67% Token Efficiency gain.
watch_item: Aggressive compression methods such as schema distillation can improve token efficiency but may introduce major semantic drift, so they need careful validation before deployment.
paper_title: Token Optimization Strategies for LLM-Based Oracle-to-PostgreSQL Migration
